metadata.title:
SENT BY TAFT
TO SEE HOW


IT'S DONE

SECRETARY DICK BALllNGER
LOOKING AFTER REClAMA-
TION NEUTRALITY-SUSPICIOUS.
AGENCIES ALSO NEED At
TENTlON.

pages.raw_text:
SEATTLE, July 13.-Richard A.
Ballinger, secretary of the interior,
is here today on an official tour to
Investigate matters connected with
the interior department. He is de-
voting particular attention to the
work of the reclamation service in
Its several projects.


Conditions at certain Indian
agencies" he said, ARE far from
satisfactory and require rigid in
vestigation. The president has also
asked me to familiarize myself
with the question of the reclama-
ton of arid lands.


THE government has spent
something like $50,000,000 from the
proceeds of the sales of public
lands for reclamation work. The
investment is so large and the
work so important that the presi'
dent wants me to conduct an in
vestigation at close range in the
same way that he sent Secretary
Dickinson to Panama to become ia
miliar with the construction of the
canal"
